mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates a synchronized collapse in consumer and investment demand, depressing corporate earnings expectations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization and output growth, supporting corporate revenue expansion and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns do not outperform baseline when CPIAUCSL remains stable, or the signal reverses direction during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 4.5% imposes severe financing constraints on highly leveraged firms, triggering margin calls and forced deleveraging that produce negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in over 60% of observations where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, invalidating the leverage constraint mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rate indicates labor force participation withdrawal rather than genuine job creation, masking underlying economic weakness and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03, or the signal becomes positive when participation rates stabilize.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth directly increases aggregate corporate revenues and profit margins, creating a persistent upward pressure on equity valuations that yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 remains below 0.02 across rolling 3-year windows, or positive GDP growth correlates with negative 20-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in industrial production during periods of falling consumer prices signal deflationary supply shocks that erode corporate pricing power and trigger immediate negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 45% of instances where INDPRO falls alongside declining CPIAUCSL, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation expectations remain anchored.
